findings.lead: GAMEOVER-01 — Terminal decimator aliasing / stranded terminal-dec payout
findings.reasoning: No aliasing counterexample found. The terminal decimator writes offsets to `decBucketOffsetPacked[lvl + 1]` after a fixed 11-denominator scan, while normal live rounds use their own level key; terminal claim and consume paths read the same `lvl + 1` slot (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:993`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1017`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1026`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1053`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1098`). The path has no external calls and no participant iteration, so the only material reverts would be impossible/overflow bounds on `uint96(poolWei)` or `uint128(totalWinnerBurn)`, both bounded by protocol accounting and FLIP supply comments (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1774`). Post-gameover claim debits packed claimable/afking first and then subtracts from `claimablePool`; the debit guards both halves before the checked pool subtraction (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:967`, `contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1231`, `contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1246`).

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-02 — Gas ceiling for terminal jackpot + handleGameOverDrain
findings.reasoning: As a gas claim, this is refuted. Worst-case composition is: one terminal drain transaction after ticket batches are already split out (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:647`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:683`); at most 32 deity refunds because symbol IDs are <32 and duplicate deity ownership is blocked (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:543`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:548`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:604`); constant 11 terminal-decimator bucket checks (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:993`); and terminal jackpot capped at 305 winners (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:196`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:248`, `contracts/libraries/JackpotBucketLib.sol:89`). The terminal jackpot pays only capped selected winners, not all ticket holders (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:1141`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:1219`). In-repo gas harnesses model the 305-winner path and the split gameover composition below EIP-7825 / 16,777,216 gas (`test/gas/AdvanceStageWorstCaseGas.t.sol:178`, `test/gas/AdvanceStageWorstCaseGas.t.sol:220`, `test/gas/GameOverCompositionAdvanceGas.t.sol:9`, `test/gas/GameOverCompositionAdvanceGas.t.sol:148`). I did not rerun tests because the sandbox is read-only.

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-02 — Terminal drain all-or-nothing retry semantics
findings.reasoning: A revert after the latch does not create a committed half-gameover state. `handleGameOverDrain` sets `gameOver=true` and `GO_TIME`, then calls sDGNRS/GNRUS/FLIP cleanup, then writes `GO_JACKPOT_PAID`, then runs terminal decimator/jackpot (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:134`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:139`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:144`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:174`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:186`). Any later revert rolls back the whole transaction, including external-call side effects, so retry starts from the prior pre-gameover state. The RNG-word gate is reached before that latch and reverts only before state is committed (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:91`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:636`).

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-02 — FLIP tombstone overflow can block terminal drain
findings.trigger: Before gameover, grow `FLIP.vaultMintAllowance()` above `type(uint128).max - 1e36`; then trigger liveness gameover so `handleGameOverDrain` calls `flip.tombstoneAtGameOver()`. The `_toUint128(vaultAllowance + FLIP_TOMBSTONE_WEI)` conversion reverts, rolling back terminal drain and preventing finalization until the allowance is reduced out-of-band.
findings.reasoning: This is the main counterexample I found. `handleGameOverDrain` calls `flip.tombstoneAtGameOver()` before jackpot-paid is committed (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:139`). FLIP tombstone adds `FLIP_TOMBSTONE_WEI` to `_supply.vaultAllowance` through `_toUint128`, which reverts on overflow (`contracts/FLIP.sol:172`, `contracts/FLIP.sol:371`, `contracts/FLIP.sol:559`). The allowance can be increased without governance by transferring FLIP to the VAULT; that path burns user balance from total supply and adds the amount to vault allowance (`contracts/FLIP.sol:338`, `contracts/FLIP.sol:384`, `contracts/FLIP.sol:400`). The magnitude is extreme, but there is no code-level invariant reserving tombstone headroom, and the existing fuzz negative control demonstrates the overflow boundary (`test/fuzz/FlipTombstone.t.sol:241`). If reachable in live economics, this blocks the terminal drain entrypoint, therefore all later gameover finalization.

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-03 — Gameover trigger / liveness bailout gating
findings.reasoning: No permanent gate-off window found. `_livenessTriggered()` intentionally returns false while `lastPurchaseDay` or `jackpotPhaseFlag` is set, preventing fallback liveness from interrupting productive last-purchase/jackpot work (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1437`, `contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1465`). Normal stage logic clears those flags through the jackpot/phase-transition path before liveness gameover becomes the way forward (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:423`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:530`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:547`). Once neither flag is set, `_handleGameOverPath` is reachable from `advanceGame` when liveness is true (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:205`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:629`).

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-03 — Final sweep sink rejection / GO_SWEPT ordering
findings.reasoning: The sweep writes `GO_SWEPT` before transfers, but a sink revert rolls that write back with the transaction, so retries are clean (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:203`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:209`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:237`). The three configured sinks are payable or stETH-transfer compatible: VAULT has a payable receive (`contracts/DegenerusVault.sol:479`), sDGNRS accepts ETH from GAME (`contracts/sDGNRS.sol:498`), and GNRUS has an unrestricted payable receive (`contracts/GNRUS.sol:651`). `_sendStethFirst` performs only ERC20 `transfer` and ETH `.call`, reverting on false/failed send (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:250`). I do not see a permanent sweep wedge under honest deployed sinks.

findings.lead: GAMEOVER-03 — Final sweep claimablePool underflow / ordering
findings.reasoning: No underflow counterexample found under the protocol accounting invariant. The sweep snapshots only the three sink claimables, debits those packed balances, zeroes `claimablePool`, then distributes total contract funds less the three already-owed amounts (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:211`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:214`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:217`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:233`). Ordinary player claims are blocked after `GO_SWEPT`, so they cannot subtract from the zeroed pool later (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1230`, `contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1275`).

The deity refund loop is bounded by the pass universe, not by arbitrary users. Purchases require `symbolId < 32`, reject duplicate symbol deity, and reject duplicate owner deity status, then push one owner (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:543`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:548`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol:604`). The public availability view uses the same `< 32` bound (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:886`). `handleGameOverDrain` loops over `deityPassOwners.length` only for `lvl < 10`, credits each refund, and adds one aggregate liability to `claimablePool` (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:99`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:106`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:126`).

findings.lead: Hotspot — Pending degenerette bets / lootboxes after liveness
findings.trigger: Have unresolved degenerette bets or unopened lootboxes when liveness gameover takes over. `resolveBets` and open paths stop processing under liveness, so those positions do not settle into per-player claimable before terminal distribution.
findings.reasoning: This is not a terminal wedge, but it refutes the stronger hotspot premise that every pending bet/lootbox is settled into claimable. `resolveBets` explicitly reverts under `_livenessTriggered()` and comments that pending bets are handled by gameover drain rather than individual resolution (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:429`). ETH bets have already put value into pending/future pools (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:593`), so funds are not stranded; they become part of terminal distribution. Lootbox auto/open paths similarly return no work under liveness (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:659`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAfkingModule.sol:1504`). Pending sDGNRS redemption is better handled: gameover entropy resolves a pending period before drain (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1310`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1345`).

findings.lead: Hotspot — Terminal jackpot winner set / terminal decimator iteration
findings.reasoning: No unbounded winner-set or decimator-iteration wedge found. Terminal jackpot uses capped bucket counts derived from the pool cap and `DAILY_ETH_MAX_WINNERS = 305` (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:196`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:248`). `_processBucket` clamps each bucket to `MAX_BUCKET_WINNERS = 250`, and random selection samples a requested winner count rather than scanning all holders (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:184`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:1141`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol:1476`). Terminal decimator scans denominators 2 through 12 only (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:993`).

newVectors.title: FLIP tombstone allowance headroom is not reserved
newVectors.trigger: Accumulate `FLIP.vaultMintAllowance() > type(uint128).max - 1e36`, then enter gameover drain.
newVectors.reasoning: `FLIP.tombstoneAtGameOver()` can revert on `uint128` overflow before terminal drain commits. This is a terminal-liveness counterexample if the extreme allowance state is economically reachable (`contracts/FLIP.sol:559`, `contracts/FLIP.sol:371`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:139`).

newVectors.title: Liveness gameover forfeits unresolved degenerette/lootbox positions rather than settling them per player
newVectors.trigger: Let unresolved bets/boxes remain until `_livenessTriggered()` is true.
newVectors.reasoning: The code intentionally blocks late resolution/opening under liveness and relies on terminal pool distribution instead; this is not a finalization wedge but is a player-accounting/economic behavior to document (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:429`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:659`).

overallVerdict: Not a clean all-refuted GAMEOVER proof. Decimator aliasing, final sweep, liveness gate reachability, deity-loop bounds, and gas composition are refuted as terminal wedges. The material terminal-liveness counterexample is FLIP tombstone overflow if vault allowance can reach the extreme headroom boundary. Separately, pending degenerette/lootbox positions are not stranded as ETH, but they are not settled into player claimable under liveness gameover.
